symbols: book held in her arm — the faith and learning of Helena, mother of Emperor Constantine
halo — her sanctity as the discoverer of the True Cross
architectural niche — her honored place in the communion of saints
barefoot stance — humility despite imperial status
description: A tall, narrow panel from a polyptych showing a young woman standing in an architectural niche with a draped canopy above. She wears a deep red-brown dress with flowing drapery. A subtle halo glows behind her head. She holds a closed book in her left arm and gestures with her right hand. She stands barefoot on a dark floor. Her expression is serene and gentle. The palette is warm, dominated by earth tones and deep reds.
theological_description: Perugino's panel depicts Saint Helena (c. 246-330), mother of Emperor Constantine the Great. Helena is venerated for her pilgrimage to the Holy Land around 326-328 AD, during which tradition holds she discovered the True Cross — the actual cross on which Jesus was crucified. Her finding of the cross connects to the central proclamation of the Christian faith: 'May I never boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ' (Galatians 6:14). Helena's journey was motivated by the same impulse that drives all Christian pilgrimage — the desire to draw near to the places where God acted in history. Her discovery of the cross affirms the material reality of the Incarnation and the Atonement: God entered real history, suffered on real wood, and was buried in a real tomb. Paul declared: 'For the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God' (1 Corinthians 1:18). Helena, though empress, traveled as a humble pilgrim — her barefoot depiction here reflecting the pattern of rulers who bow before the cross. Her legacy connects to the broader theme that God uses unlikely agents — a woman in an age of male dominance, an elderly pilgrim — to preserve and reveal the instruments of salvation.

scripture_refs.reason: Boasting only in the cross of Christ — Helena's devotion to finding the True Cross
The message of the cross as the power of God — the theological significance of Helena's discovery
Christ's obedience to death on a cross — the cross Helena sought was the instrument of this obedience
